name: Bagels Sesame Garlic or Plain
recipeIngredient: 1/2 tablespoon to 1 tablespoon Garlic powder (to taste)
recipeInstructions:
From The Honey Bear Bakery, Kalispell, Montana. Makes 8 sizable bagels.
Eggless. A handsome bagel. Double the recipe if you expect company - they'll disappear fast. Omit the sesame and garlic for plain bagels.
1. Mix together yeast, honey, warm water, and add hard whole wheat flour until well combined. Let sit until bubbly.
2. Add remaining ingredients to sponge and knead dough well.
3. Cover and let dough rise in an oiled bowl until doubled in size.
4. Punch down dough and divide into 8 pieces.
5. Bring a large pan of water to boil. (Optional) Add 1/2 tablespoon honey to the boiling water.)
6. Shape each piece into round rolls, then push thumbs through the center to create a hole.
7. Drop bagels into boiling water immediately after shaping.
8. Cook for about 1 minute or until they puff up fast.
9. Remove from water and place on an oiled pan.
10. Bake at 350°F for 20-25 minutes or at 400°F for 10-15 minutes, until lightly browned.
